name: Self-Determination Theory (Autonomy / Competence / Relatedness)
definition_origin: Edward L. Deci and Richard M. Ryan (University of Rochester) — formal articulation in the 1980s, foundational papers Deci 1971 and 1975, codified in 'Intrinsic Motivation and Self-Determination in Human Behavior' (1985, Plenum). Major synthesis: Ryan & Deci (2000) 'Self-Determination Theory and the Facilitation of Intrinsic Motivation, Social Development, and Well-Being' (American Psychologist). Applied to UX by Nielsen Norman Group (Tanner Kohler, January 2022, 'Autonomy, Relatedness, and Competence in UX Design'). Yu-kai Chou and the gamification community apply SDT extensively in product design.
core_mechanism: Humans have three innate, universal psychological needs whose satisfaction is the substrate of intrinsic motivation and well-being: AUTONOMY (volition — feeling actions are self-chosen, not coerced), COMPETENCE (mastery — feeling capable of producing desired outcomes), RELATEDNESS (belonging — feeling cared-for and connected). Products that satisfy all three produce sustained, high-quality engagement; products that satisfy only extrinsic needs (rewards, points, social pressure) produce shallow engagement that decays as soon as the reward is withdrawn. SDT also distinguishes a motivation continuum from amotivation through controlled extrinsic to autonomous intrinsic — only the autonomous end produces durable well-being. Critically, SDT is the strongest theoretical separator between ethical engagement (need-satisfaction) and manipulation (need-thwarting via FOMO/streak guilt).
neuroscience_correlate: Intrinsic motivation correlates with striatal/ventral-tegmental dopaminergic activity (Murayama et al. 2010 PNAS — fMRI showed extrinsic reward undermines intrinsic motivation circuits). Autonomy support associates with reduced amygdala threat response and increased medial prefrontal self-referential processing. Relatedness engages oxytocin/social-bonding circuits. The neural distinctness of the three needs is supported but not as cleanly mapped as SDT's behavioral data.
empirical_evidence: (1) Ryan & Deci (2000) American Psychologist — foundational synthesis, 10,000+ citations. (2) Wang et al. (2024) self-determination meta-analysis on educational outcomes — confirmed need-support predicts academic outcomes, well-being, and persistence (selfdeterminationtheory.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/2024_WangWangEtAl_MetaEdu.pdf). (3) Springer 2024 (Springer TechTrends, 'Advancing Gamification Research and Practice with Three Underexplored Ideas in SDT') — explicit SDT-aligned gamification framework. (4) Wiley/Springer meta-analyses on gamification: pooled g ≈ 0.654, with autonomy/relatedness gains larger than competence gains; intrinsic motivation g ≈ 0.638. (5) Cross-cultural: Nalipay, King & Cai (2020) 'Autonomy is equally important across East and West' — autonomy predicted well-being similarly in collectivist (PH, ID, MY) and individualist samples, refuting earlier critiques. (6) Chirkov, Ryan, Kim & Kaplan (2003) differentiated autonomy from individualism. (7) ACM TOCHI 2024 'SDT and HCI Games Research: Unfulfilled Promises and Unquestioned Paradigms' — flags measurement issues but confirms framework's HCI applicability.
measurable_outcome_metric: Intrinsic Motivation Inventory (IMI) score, Basic Psychological Need Satisfaction Scale (BPNS), self-reported autonomy/competence/relatedness Likert items, D7 / D30 retention, voluntary use frequency (vs. notification-driven), unsubscribe and uninstall rates, qualitative 'why I keep using this' interview themes, NPS, paid-conversion among non-FOMO-pressured cohorts.
engineering_tactics: AUTONOMY: (1) make every primary feature opt-in, never default to maximum notifications; (2) expose user-configurable goals (Duolingo daily XP target picker is canonical); (3) provide clear opt-outs for streaks/notifications/social features; (4) avoid forced single-path flows — Linear's keyboard-first design is the autonomy-respecting pattern. COMPETENCE: (1) progressive challenge — Eyal's 'minimum viable habit' onboarding plus Headspace's incremental session lengths; (2) immediate feedback (sensoryFeedback on every primary action); (3) visible mastery progress (skill bars, level badges) — but tie them to actual skill, not vanity; (4) just-in-time tutorials (Otter pattern) over upfront wizards. RELATEDNESS: (1) human-feeling microcopy (Mailchimp Voice & Tone, Slack loading messages) — the product itself can be a relatedness object; (2) co-presence features (Strava kudos, Duolingo friend quests) without forced public ranking; (3) authentic mascot-led messages (Apple Watch encouragement) avoid robotic templates. Hard rule: SDT-aligned engagement should withstand the test 'would the user thank us for nudging them?'
real_world_examples: (1) Duolingo — autonomy via daily-goal picker, competence via XP/skill tree, relatedness via friend quests and league chat. Mixed ethics: streak-loss notifications can flip from competence-supportive to controlled extrinsic (criticized 2024). (2) Apple Watch — autonomy via customizable goals, competence via ring-close progression, relatedness via Activity Sharing 'kudos'. (3) Strava — autonomy in workout type/route choice, competence in PRs, relatedness in club kudos and segment leaderboards. (4) Headspace — autonomy via session-length picker, competence via streak + completed sessions visible, relatedness via teacher's voice as a parasocial relatedness object. (5) Notion — high autonomy (configurable workspace), competence (templates teach mastery), relatedness (collaborative comments). (6) Mimo (coding) — explicitly cited by NN/g for letting users choose learning order. ANTI-EXAMPLES (need-thwarting): (a) Justanswer.com forcing chat-only interaction (NN/g cite); (b) Peel sending four promo SMS in a week (NN/g cite); (c) FileZilla offering no onboarding guidance (competence-thwarting).
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile gives stronger relatedness signals via lock-screen widgets, push notifications (used carefully), live activities (iOS 16.1+), and Android Live Updates — but the same channels are the most easily abused for need-thwarting. Web has weaker relatedness channels (no lock-screen, no haptic) but stronger autonomy via keyboard-first power-user flows (Linear, Raycast, Vercel command bars). Both require the same SDT discipline; mobile just has more rope to hang yourself with.
platform_design_language_alignment: Aligned with both 2025-2026 platform languages. Apple HIG explicitly emphasizes user control (autonomy) and 'people-first design'. Liquid Glass leans into expressive feedback (competence). Material 3 Expressive 18k-participant research included engagement and well-being among measured outcomes — its expressive feedback boosts perceived competence by accelerating recognition speed up to 4x. SDT alignment is now ship-faster guidance, especially given regulatory pressure from DFA/AADC.
claude_design_application: Instruct Claude to design need-supporting, not need-thwarting, mechanics. Example prompt fragment 1: 'For the daily-goal picker, support AUTONOMY: 4 preset levels (Casual / Regular / Serious / Insane) plus a custom slider, default to Regular not Insane, easy to change anytime from settings. Support COMPETENCE: visual progress bar + sensoryFeedback .success on goal complete, milestone celebration at 7/30/100 days. Support RELATEDNESS: optional friend-quest invite, OFF by default, no public leaderboard ranking unless explicitly opted-in.' Example prompt fragment 2: 'Audit notification copy for need-thwarting language. Forbid loss-framed messaging like Don\'t lose your streak! or You\'re falling behind your friends. Replace with autonomy-respecting language like Ready when you are or A 5-minute session is waiting. Provide a clear notification preferences screen with granular opt-outs for each notification kind.'
stitch_application: Stitch needs explicit SDT cues because it can produce manipulative gamification by default. Example prompt fragment 1: 'Design an onboarding goal-setting screen with explicit AUTONOMY support: 4 preset cards (Casual 5 min, Regular 15 min, Serious 30 min, Insane 60 min) with a 5th Custom option. Default selection on Regular. Skip CTA visible at top-right. Microcopy: You can change this anytime. Avoid pressure-language like Choose your commitment or Are you serious about your goals?' Example prompt fragment 2: 'Friends/social tab with RELATEDNESS without coercion: small list of friends with kudos buttons (heart icon), no public leaderboard, no ranking number, no Compare-to-friends widget. Header copy: People cheering you on. Forbid red urgency badges and Friends are ahead of you messaging.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen reaches for the most engagement-extracting gamification by default — slot-machine variable rewards, FOMO countdown timers, public leaderboards, streak-loss notifications, default-on social pressure. This is need-thwarting and increasingly DFA/AADC risk. Counter-prompt: 'Apply Self-Determination Theory ethically. Forbid: streak-loss guilt notifications, default-on public leaderboards, FOMO countdown timers, slot-machine variable rewards. Require: opt-in for every social feature, granular notification controls, autonomy-respecting microcopy (no Don\'t lose your..., no You\'re falling behind), and a competence-supporting (not vanity) progress display. Add a code comment block listing how each UI element supports autonomy/competence/relatedness or flag it for removal.'
anti_patterns: (1) Streak-loss notifications that frame loss as moral failure — flips competence-support into controlled extrinsic motivation and toward DFA/AADC dark-pattern territory. (2) Default-on public leaderboards — thwarts autonomy and creates relatedness-via-shame. (3) Forced social signup (Login with Friends required) — autonomy violation. (4) Slot-machine variable rewards on top of existing competence systems — undermines intrinsic motivation per Murayama et al. 2010. (5) Generic robotic microcopy ('Action successful') — relatedness opportunity wasted. (6) Overwhelming first-session settings — competence-thwarting. (7) Manipulative re-engagement notifications timed to peak guilt windows.
regulatory_risk: Medium. SDT itself is benign — its ethical scaffolding is what reduces regulatory exposure of other tactics (gamification, streaks, notifications). Risk arises when products invoke the language of SDT while implementing need-thwarting mechanics — e.g. claiming 'we support your autonomy' while defaulting all notifications on. EU Digital Fairness Act (DFA, finalizing 2026), DSA dark-pattern rules, UK AADC, California AADC all explicitly target need-thwarting design (default-on engagement loops, loss-framed nudges, default public profiles for minors). BEUC SHEIN complaint (June 2025) is the precedent. Mitigate by designing default settings that favor user well-being and granular opt-outs.
pitfalls_caveats: (1) SDT can be cargo-culted — products advertise 'autonomy-supportive' while shipping coercive defaults. (2) Need-satisfaction is contextual — the same feature can support autonomy for one user and thwart it for another (e.g., daily reminders). (3) Over-customization can paradoxically reduce competence (Hick's Law collision) — autonomy needs sensible defaults. (4) Cross-cultural calibration on autonomy-as-individualism vs. autonomy-as-volition (Chirkov et al. 2003 distinguished these). (5) Measurement: BPNS and IMI scales have known cross-cultural psychometric issues; use multi-scale + qualitative triangulation. (6) Some HCI critics (ACM TOCHI 2024) note SDT in HCI is sometimes invoked without rigorous operationalization.
cross_cultural_validity: Strongly validated cross-culturally. Nalipay, King & Cai (2020, ScienceDirect) tested SDT universality in 12 countries — autonomy predicted well-being equally well in East and West, refuting the older critique that autonomy was a Western individualist construct. Chirkov, Ryan, Kim & Kaplan (2003) differentiated autonomy (volition) from individualism (independence) — the former is universal, the latter is culture-specific. Practical calibration: relatedness register varies (US warmer, JP/KR more reserved, AR more familial); competence display varies (public ranking is more accepted in CN, EU users prefer private progress). RTL languages need mirrored progress indicators. Confirmed valid; tactical execution must localize.
sources: [Ryan & Deci (2000) — SDT and the Facilitation of Intrinsic Motivation (PDF)](https://selfdeterminationtheory.org/SDT/documents/2000_RyanDeci_SDT.pdf)
[Autonomy, Relatedness, and Competence in UX Design — Nielsen Norman Group (Tanner Kohler, 2022)](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/autonomy-relatedness-competence/)
[Wang et al. (2024) — SDT meta-analysis on educational outcomes](https://selfdeterminationtheory.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/2024_WangWangEtAl_MetaEdu.pdf)
[Nalipay, King & Cai (2020) — Autonomy is equally important across East and West](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0140197119302295)
[ACM TOCHI 2024 — SDT and HCI Games Research: Unfulfilled Promises](https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3673230)
[Yu-kai Chou — SDT Guide to Ryan & Deci's Motivation Framework](https://yukaichou.com/gamification-analysis/self-determination-theory-guide-to-ryan-and-decis-motivation-framework/)
